Title: Deletion and tandem duplications of biosynthetic genes drive the diversity of triterpenoids in Aralia elata

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: does not contribute to dammarenediol biosynthesis; instead functions as a multifunctional α/β-amyrin synthase and is not expressed in tested A. elata tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: not expressed in any tested A. elata tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS/GC-MS product analysis; expression analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: not a useful DDS substitute for restoring dammarane-type saponin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Although similar to Centella asiatica DDS, AeAS3 is not a DDS. 2. Recombinant AeAS3 produces α- and β-amyrin but not dammarenediol II. 3. Lack of expression further excludes it from endogenous dammarane biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally tested and ruled out as DDS-like candidate.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high expression in root, stem, and leaf tissues of Panax species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously overexpressed in A. elata callus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: complementation/overexpression in A. elata callus; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: restores accumulation of dammarenediol II and hydroxylated dammarane-type derivatives in A. elata callus
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PgDDS expression in A. elata callus reconstructed dammarenediol II accumulation. 2. It rescued production of hydroxylated dammarane-type triterpenes, showing that loss of endogenous DDS explains absence of dammarane-type saponins in A. elata. 3. DDS is the key missing enzymatic step separating Panax and Aralia triterpenoid profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: callus in complementation assay; naturally roots and leaves in Panax species
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA755350; genome project: PRJCA006215
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for engineering A. elata or yeast to recover dammarane-type saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene from source species Panax ginseng was used to complement missing endogenous DDS function in A. elata.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: expected to cyclize 2,3-oxidosqualene to dammarenediol II, but endogenous gene is incomplete/pseudogenized
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: incomplete ortholog with only 5 exons; not functional
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: synteny analysis; exon structure comparison with PnDDS/PgDDS; metabolite absence and complementation by PgDDS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of function causes failure to synthesize dammarane-type saponins in A. elata
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A. elata contains only an incomplete DDS ortholog with 5 exons versus 17 exons in PnDDS. 2. Deletion of 12 exons is associated with absence of dammarane-type triterpenes in all tested tissues. 3. Complementation by PgDDS supports AeDDS pseudogenization as causal.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA755350; genome project: PRJCA006215
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: diagnostic target for restoring dammarane-type saponin biosynthesis in Aralia
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers ancestral DDS pseudogenization after divergence from Panax.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in leaves of A. elata; heterologously expressed in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS detection of β-amyrin; pathway reconstruction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: initiates oleanane-type triterpene skeleton biosynthesis and supports de novo araloside production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeBAS1 encodes a functional β-amyrin synthase. 2. It shows high expression in leaves, matching oleanane-type pathway activity. 3. Combined with downstream enzymes, it enables production of multiple aralosides in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves and other tissues containing oleanane-type triterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AeCYP716A354; AeCYP716A355; AeCYP72A935; AeCYP72A936; AeCYP72A937; AeCSLM1; AeUGT73CB3; AeUGT74AG6
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: core entry-point gene for engineering araloside biosynthesis in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as coexpression bait to identify candidate P450 tailoring genes.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS detection of β-amyrin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: produces β-amyrin as an alternative BAS enzyme in A. elata
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeBAS2 also encodes a functional β-amyrin synthase. 2. It has high amino acid similarity to AeBAS1. 3. It contributes to β-amyrin-forming capacity in A. elata.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: alternative BAS module for triterpene engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less emphasized than AeBAS1 in pathway reconstruction.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes the C28 position of β-amyrin to form oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: coexpressed with AeBAS1; high in leaves; active in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression analysis; phylogenetic analysis; heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: produces oleanolic acid precursor for diverse oleanane-type saponins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeCYP716A354 catalyzes C28 oxidation of β-amyrin to oleanolic acid. 2. It is highly expressed in leaves and coexpressed with AeBAS1. 3. It is a key entry tailoring enzyme for downstream araloside diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: essential oxidative entry enzyme for araloside pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous gene in P. notoginseng had very low expression.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates oleanolic acid at C16 to produce echinocystic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in leaves; active in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression analysis; phylogenetic analysis; heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: directs flux from oleanolic acid to echinocystic acid branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeCYP716A355 functions as a C16 hydroxylase of oleanolic acid. 2. It generates echinocystic acid in yeast. 3. Its tissue expression supports branch diversification of A. elata pentacyclic triterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for engineering echinocystic acid-type aralosides
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous gene expression in P. notoginseng was extremely low.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes/hydroxylates dammarenediol II to form protopanaxadiol
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed ubiquitously in all tested organs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: demonstrates that downstream dammarane tailoring capacity is retained in A. elata
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeCYP716A357 converts dammarenediol II to protopanaxadiol. 2. It is ubiquitously expressed in A. elata. 3. Conservation of this enzyme indicates that missing DDS, not downstream tailoring steps, explains absence of dammarane-type saponins.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: supports engineering of dammarane-type products in A. elata if DDS is restored
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as evidence that dammarane tailoring enzymes are conserved.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates the C23 position of oleanolic acid and echinocystic acid to form hederagenin and 16-OH-hederagenin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phylogenetic analysis; tandem duplication/synteny analysis; heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS; NMR confirmation of product class
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: expands oleanane-type skeleton diversity by C23 hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeCYP72A935 is a tandem-duplicated CYP72A enzyme specific to Aralia lineage. 2. It hydroxylates oleanolic acid and echinocystic acid at C23. 3. It contributes to hederagenin- and 16-OH-hederagenin-type triterpenoid diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: target for generating hederagenin-rich or 16-OH-hederagenin-rich chemotypes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of two tandem duplicates with similar catalytic capacity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates the C23 position of oleanolic acid and echinocystic acid to form hederagenin and 16-OH-hederagenin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: tandem duplication/synteny analysis; heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports diversification of oleanane skeletons and was used in multigene yeast reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeCYP72A936 is a tandem duplicate of AeCYP72A935 with similar catalytic activity. 2. It forms hederagenin and 16-OH-hederagenin through C23 hydroxylation. 3. It was incorporated into engineered yeast to generate multiple aralosides.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: engineering module for hederagenin/16-OH-hederagenin branch expansion
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inserted into strain WEA for pathway extension.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: coexpressed with AeBAS1 among candidate P450s
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coexpression analysis; phylogenetic analysis; heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeCYP72A937 was functionally assigned as a hederagenin C16 hydroxylase. 2. It contributes to one of the major pentacyclic skeletons in A. elata. 3. It broadens downstream araloside structural diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text contains a likely figure-text inconsistency naming 935/936 in one legend, but Results explicitly assign 937 to hederagenin hydroxylation.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C3-glucuronosylated triterpenes including calenduloside E and echinocystic acid 3-O-glucuronopyranoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers glucuronic acid to the C3 position of oleanane-type triterpene aglycones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: different from AeCSLM2; specific detail not fully stated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: tandem duplication/synteny analysis; heterologous expression in yeast with EcUGD1; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables C3 glucuronidation, producing calenduloside E and related glucuronides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeCSLM1 is a tandem-duplicated CSLM involved in triterpene 3-O-glucuronosylation. 2. It modifies multiple oleanane-type skeletons. 3. It is essential for biosynthesis of glucuronidated aralosides in engineered yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: requires UDP-glucuronic acid supply via EcUGD1 in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: critical glycosylation module for producing medicinal aralosides in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of an A. elata-specific tandem expansion relative to P. notoginseng.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers glucuronic acid to the C3 position of oleanane-type triterpene aglycones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: tandem duplication/synteny analysis; heterologous expression in yeast with EcUGD1; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: adds C3 glucuronic acid to four different oleanane-type skeletons
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeCSLM2 is functionally redundant with AeCSLM1 for triterpene C3 glucuronidation. 2. It arose from tandem duplication in A. elata. 3. Divergent expression patterns may contribute to tissue-specific araloside accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: requires UDP-glucuronic acid supply via EcUGD1 in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: alternative C3 glucuronosylation module for pathway engineering

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Aralia elata; Escherichia coli; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Aralia elata; Escherichia coli; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: adds glucose to the C3-linked glucuronic acid of calenduloside E to form zingibroside R1 through a 1→2 glycosidic bond
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: zingibroside R1 and related C3-diglycosylated aralosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGEX-4T-1 in E. coli for in vitro assay; GAL-inducible pESC in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: active in vitro and in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; tandem duplication analysis; heterologous yeast reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: efficiently catalyzes C3 glucosyl extension, especially on calenduloside E, producing zingibroside R1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeUGT73CB3 glucosylates calenduloside E at C3 to form zingibroside R1. 2. It shows higher affinity and efficiency for calenduloside E than for chikusetsusaponin IVa. 3. It is a major contributor to A. elata-specific glycosylation diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable for producing specific medicinal aralosides such as zingibroside R1 in synthetic biology platforms
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Turnover higher than tandem duplicates AeUGT73CB4/5.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: adds glucose to calenduloside E at the C3 glycoside through a 1→2 linkage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expression pattern differs from AeUGT73CB3 and is lower emphasis than leaf-high CB3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; tandem duplication analysis; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: catalyzes the same reaction as AeUGT73CB3 but with lower turnover
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeUGT73CB4 arose from tandem duplication after divergence from P. notoginseng. 2. It forms zingibroside R1 from calenduloside E. 3. Its catalytic efficiency is substantially lower than AeUGT73CB3.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: lower activity may limit contribution to metabolite accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: secondary enzyme option for glycosylation engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Duplicated copies listed as AE09G01562 and AE09G01563 in text.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: adds glucose to calenduloside E at the C3 glycoside through a 1→2 linkage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; tandem duplication analysis; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: performs C3 glucosyl extension with lower turnover than AeUGT73CB3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeUGT73CB5 is another tandemly duplicated UGT73. 2. It glucosylates calenduloside E to produce zingibroside R1. 3. Its activity is lower than AeUGT73CB3, suggesting subfunctionalized or minor contribution.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: minor alternative catalyst for metabolic engineering

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: four pentacyclic triterpene skeletons including oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C28-glucosylated pentacyclic triterpenes such as oleanolic acid 28-O-glucopyranosyl ester
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: glycosylates the C28 position of pentacyclic triterpene skeletons
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; synteny analysis; kinetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: catalyzes C28 glycosylation of aglycones but not C3-glucuronidated substrates such as calenduloside E or zingibroside R1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeUGT73AD2 is the only active member in its conserved UGT73 cluster for pentacyclic triterpene C28 glycosylation. 2. It acts on multiple aglycone skeletons. 3. It cannot glycosylate calenduloside E or zingibroside R1, implying additional root-expressed UGTs are required.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: substrate scope excludes some key glucuronidated intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for producing C28-glucosylated aglycone derivatives but insufficient alone for full araloside pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cluster is conserved in Panax and Aralia.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Aralia elata; Escherichia coli; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Aralia elata; Escherichia coli; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C28 glycosylation module for C3-glucuronidated substrates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: calenduloside E; zingibroside R1; oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: chikusetsusaponin IVa and related C28-glucosylated products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: glycosylates the C28 position of oleanane-type pentacyclic triterpenes including C3-glucuronidated intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: chikusetsusaponin IVa and highly glycosylated aralosides
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGEX-4T-1 in E. coli for assay; GAL-inducible pESC in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: specifically expressed at high levels in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root-enriched in A. elata; used in yeast reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression screening; in vitro enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; heterologous yeast reconstruction; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: completes C28 glycosylation of glucuronidated intermediates, enabling abundant root-type saponins such as chikusetsusaponin IVa
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AeUGT74AG6 was identified from root-enriched UGTs as a C28 glycosyltransferase acting on C3-glucuronidated triterpenes. 2. It shows activity toward calenduloside E and zingibroside R1 and high catalytic efficiency toward zingibroside R1. 3. It enabled formation of terminal glycosylated aralosides in engineered yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong terminal-step gene for engineering high-value aralosides, especially root-type C28-glycosylated saponins
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Proposed to compensate for glycosyltransferase requirement at C28 in roots.
